axes.name: Economy
axes.left_description: Redistribution, strong public services, government economic intervention
axes.right_description: Market competition, privatization, low taxes, business freedom

axes.name: Social Liberty
axes.left_description: Strong social norms, order, state guidance on personal conduct
axes.right_description: Maximum personal freedom, minimal social coercion

axes.name: Globalism vs Nationalism
axes.right_description: International cooperation, open borders, free trade

axes.name: Institutional Trust
axes.left_description: Skepticism of elites, people vs establishment
axes.right_description: Trust in experts, media, courts, bureaucracy

axes.name: Science & Public Health
axes.left_description: Low trust in health institutions, anti-mandate, individual choice
axes.right_description: Trust in medical science, evidence-based policy, public health mandates

axes.name: Technology & AI
axes.left_description: Strong AI regulation, safety-first, labor protection
axes.right_description: Rapid AI development, tech innovation, minimal regulation

axes.name: Climate & Energy
axes.left_description: Slower transition, cost focus, fossil fuels, techno-optimism without mandates
axes.right_description: Aggressive climate policy, renewables, possible nuclear

axes.name: Information & Truth
axes.left_description: Opposition to censorship, skepticism of truth enforcement, open discourse
axes.right_description: Support moderation to reduce misinformation, platform accountability

questions.text: The government should redistribute wealth from the rich to the poor.
Privatization improves the efficiency of public services.
High taxes on businesses hurt economic growth.
Universal public services like Medicare are more important than keeping taxes low.
Strong unions are essential for protecting workers' rights.
The free market, left alone, produces the best outcomes for society.
Governments should cap CEO pay relative to average worker wages.
Red tape and regulation are the biggest obstacles to prosperity.
The government has no right to tell adults what they can do with their own bodies.
Society needs stronger moral standards enforced by law.
Drug use should be decriminalised. It's a personal choice, not a crime.
Traditional family structures should be actively promoted by government policy.
People should be free to express themselves however they want, even if others find it offensive.
Law and order should take priority over individual privacy rights.
Same-sex marriage and gender identity rights are non-negotiable civil liberties.
Surveillance cameras and police powers should be expanded to keep communities safe.
Australia should prioritise its own interests over international obligations.
Global problems like climate change require binding international agreements.
Immigration should be significantly reduced to protect local jobs and culture.
Free trade agreements benefit ordinary people, not just corporations.
Buying Australian-made products should be encouraged even if they cost more.
Refugees fleeing war and persecution deserve protection regardless of where they come from.
Multiculturalism has weakened national identity.
Countries should work together through organisations like the UN rather than going it alone.
The mainstream media cannot be trusted to report honestly.
Scientists and public health experts should have significant influence on government policy.
Career politicians are out of touch with ordinary people.
Courts and the judiciary should remain independent from political pressure.
The 'establishment' works against the interests of everyday Australians.
Public servants and bureaucrats generally act in the public interest.
A political outsider would do a better job running the country than experienced politicians.
Democratic institutions, even when imperfect, are worth defending.
Public health mandates (e.g. vaccines, masks) are justified when they protect society.
Individual choice should always override public health mandates.
Medical science is generally trustworthy when forming government policy.
Big Pharma puts profits ahead of public health.
Childhood vaccination programs should be mandatory for school enrolment.
Natural remedies are often safer and more effective than pharmaceutical treatments.
Governments should fund public health campaigns based on the best available evidence.
Doctors and health authorities have too much power over people's personal decisions.
AI development should move as fast as possible. Regulation will only hold us back.
Governments should impose strict safety regulations on AI before it's widely deployed.
Automation and AI will create more jobs than they destroy in the long run.
Tech companies like Google and Meta have too much power and need breaking up.
Australia should invest heavily in becoming a global tech and AI hub.
Workers whose jobs are replaced by AI deserve guaranteed retraining and income support.
Facial recognition and AI surveillance should be banned in public spaces.
Technological progress is the best solution to most of society's problems.
Australia should aim for net zero emissions as quickly as possible, regardless of cost.
Coal and gas should remain part of Australia's energy mix for decades to come.
Nuclear energy should be part of Australia's clean energy future.
Climate change policies shouldn't hurt household budgets or cost of living.
Governments should ban new fossil fuel projects and redirect funding to renewables.
The economic costs of aggressive climate action outweigh the environmental benefits.
Electric vehicles should be subsidised to speed up the transition away from petrol.
Individual lifestyle changes matter less than what corporations and governments do on climate.
Social media platforms should be legally required to remove harmful misinformation.
People should be free to say almost anything online, even if it's false or hateful.
Governments should not decide what is true or false for the public.
Fact-checking organisations play a valuable role in public discourse.
Section 18C-style hate speech laws strike the right balance between free speech and protection.
Censorship, even of dangerous ideas, always does more harm than good.
News outlets should be held legally accountable for publishing false information.
The best response to bad speech is more speech, not regulation or bans.
